projectDescription: An AI-powered browser automation toolkit and CLI built on Playwright for building, maintaining, and debugging browser automation workflows.
keyFeatures: Live browser inspection with token-efficient AI-powered snapshot analysis
Network traffic capture to reverse-engineer site APIs
Action recording and replay for building automation scripts
Interactive debugging with pause/resume workflow support
Multi-provider AI support (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Vertex)
TypeScript library with recovery, extraction, and instrumentation APIs
Session management with persistent browser profiles
Playwright-based automation with ghost cursor visualization
publicApiSurface: workflow(handler) — define a reusable browser automation workflow
launchBrowser(args) — launch a Playwright browser session
extractFromPage(options) — AI-powered structured data extraction from pages
pageRequest(page, config) — execute typed fetch calls inside browser context
attemptWithRecovery(page, fn) — run automation with automatic popup recovery
executeRecoveryAgent(page, instruction) — run an LLM recovery agent on a page
instrumentPage(page, options) — add visualization and error enrichment to a page
instrumentContext(context, options) — instrument all pages in a browser context
installInstrumentation(page, options) — in-place page instrumentation
pause(session) — pause a workflow for interactive debugging
Logger / defaultLogger — structured logging with pluggable sinks
createLLMClientFromModel(model) — create an AI SDK LLM client
downloadViaClick(page, options) — trigger and capture file downloads
downloadAndSave(page, options) — download and persist files to disk
npx libretto init — first-time workspace setup
npx libretto open <url> — launch browser and open URL
npx libretto snapshot — capture and analyze page with AI
npx libretto exec <code> — run Playwright TypeScript against open page
npx libretto run <file> <export> — execute a workflow file
npx libretto resume — resume a paused workflow
npx libretto network — view captured network requests
npx libretto actions — view recorded user/agent actions
npx libretto save <domain> — save browser session for reuse
npx libretto connect <url> — attach to existing CDP endpoint
npx libretto ai configure <provider> — configure AI snapshot model
